zh.siteSubtitle: 澳洲原住民万年口传智慧
zh.homeIntro1: 梦幻时代（Dreamtime / Tjukurpa / Jukurrpa）是澳洲原住民的宇宙观核心概念，指的是一个超越时间的创世纪元——过去、现在与未来在其中共存。这不是线性历史中的某个时期，而是一个永恒的、持续运作的精神维度。
zh.homeIntro2: 在梦幻时代的叙事中，祖灵（Ancestral Beings）塑造了大地、河流、山脉和一切生灵，并通过歌之路（Songlines）将整片大陆编织成一张精神地图。这些故事不仅是创世神话，更是法律、生态知识和行为准则的载体，代代口传至今已超过一万年。
zh.homeIntro3: 虽然原住民传统中不存在西方意义上的「预言」概念，但梦幻时代的诸多教导包含了深刻的警示：如果人类不遵守与大地的契约——不尊重火、水、动物和土地的法则——灾难将会降临。这些万年智慧在当代环境危机面前，展现出惊人的预见性。
zh.coreQuote: 「我们不拥有土地，土地拥有我们。如果你不照顾大地，大地就不会照顾你。」
keyPointsList: 彩虹蛇 —— 水的守护者，违背其法则将招致洪水或干旱
火的法则 —— 原住民用「冷火」管理土地已有数万年历史
歌之路 —— 穿越大陆的精神地图，记录每一处地形的故事与法则
大地照护 —— 人类对土地负有神圣责任，破坏土地即破坏自身
陌生人的到来 —— 多个部落口传中预见了外来者将带来巨变
sourceList: 教导内容综合自人类学文献、原住民长老公开访谈及学术研究（Wikipedia: Dreamtime）
火焰管理相关研究参考 Bill Gammage《The Biggest Estate on Earth》（Wikipedia）
验证评价基于环境科学研究、历史记录和气候数据
编者观点不代表学术定论，亦不代表任何特定原住民社群的立场
站点图标采用同心圆——澳洲原住民点画艺术中最核心的符号，代表营地、水源、聚会场所或梦幻时代的神圣地点

en.homeIntro1: The Dreamtime (Tjukurpa / Jukurrpa) is the foundational cosmological concept of Australian Aboriginal peoples — a timeless era of creation where past, present, and future coexist. It is not a period in linear history, but an eternal, ever-present spiritual dimension.
en.homeIntro2: In Dreamtime narratives, Ancestral Beings shaped the land, rivers, mountains, and all living things, weaving the entire continent into a spiritual map through Songlines. These stories are not merely creation myths — they encode law, ecological knowledge, and codes of conduct, passed down through oral tradition for over 10,000 years.
en.homeIntro3: While Aboriginal tradition does not contain 'prophecy' in the Western sense, many Dreamtime teachings carry profound warnings: if humans fail to uphold their covenant with the land — disrespecting the laws of fire, water, animals, and earth — catastrophe will follow. This ancient wisdom has proven startlingly prescient in the face of modern environmental crisis.
en.coreQuote: "We don't own the land, the land owns us. If you don't look after Country, Country won't look after you."
keyPointsList: Rainbow Serpent —— Guardian of water; violating its laws brings flood or drought
Fire Law —— Aboriginal people managed land with 'cool fire' for tens of thousands of years
Songlines —— Spiritual maps across the continent, encoding the story and law of every landform
Care for Country —— Humans bear sacred responsibility for the land; to harm it is to harm oneself
Coming of Strangers —— Multiple tribal oral traditions foresaw outsiders bringing great upheaval
sourceList: Teachings compiled from anthropological literature, public interviews with Aboriginal elders, and academic research (Wikipedia: Dreamtime)
Fire management research references Bill Gammage's The Biggest Estate on Earth (Wikipedia)
Verification based on environmental science research, historical records, and climate data
Editorial opinions do not represent academic consensus, nor the position of any specific Aboriginal community
Site icon: concentric circles — the most fundamental symbol in Aboriginal dot painting art, representing campsites, waterholes, meeting places, or sacred Dreamtime sites
